data.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 822a "Honest bidirectional round-trip probe; de-encode 'expected-bug' guards"), cross-checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/8937/fe57). All 7 cross-child interaction modes checked under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter. VERDICT: clean pairing — no G4 finding.

7-mode walk for this pairing:
- Implicit shared state — BENIGN. 822a's file_impact is scripts/jira-pressure-test/e2e_field_validation_probe.sh (de-encode guards) + tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/test_roundtrip.py (new). No sibling touches the probe script or test_roundtrip.py; the outbound-applier cluster (33d0/2359) and the schema/fixture/contract files (8937→_snapshot_schema.py/test_snapshot_contract.py; fe57→_fakes.py/fixtures; 59ef→test_acliclient_contract.py) are all disjoint from 822a's edits. No shared-file co-edit.
- Conflicting assumptions — BENIGN. 822a assumes comments/links/parent round-trip "through the production path"; consistent with fe57 (fixtures flow through fetch_snapshot unmassaged), 8937 (snapshot-entry schema), and 33d0 (production batch applier). The outbound labels list-vs-count telemetry mismatch tracked on 2359/33d0 is outbound-telemetry-internal and untouched by 822a's round-trip assertions.
- Dependency gap — BENIGN. 822a's only input dependency — story A's fixtures/fake — is correctly declared via depends_on fe57. The producer↔consumer schema/contract (8937) is explicitly scoped OUT and is not required to stand up a behavioral round-trip. No unmet "fix-must-land-first" edge to a sibling.
- Scope overlap — BENIGN. 33d0 (outbound-only consolidation correctness) and 8937 (assembled snapshot-entry contract through fetcher→differ→applier, test_snapshot_contract.py) vs 822a (BIDIRECTIONAL inbound↔outbound comment/link/parent ROUND-TRIP, test_roundtrip.py) exercise the production path from distinct angles — distinct files, distinct directionality. 822a explicitly scopes schema/contract OUT (→8937). Complementary, not duplicative.
- Ordering violation — BENIGN. 822a's edited files are disjoint from the 33d0↔2359 same-region outbound-applier sequencing cluster. The only ordering edge (fixtures before the test that uses them) is captured by depends_on fe57.
- Consumer impact — BENIGN. 822a AC3 keeps the hermetic round-trip in CI and any live variant opt-in/scheduled/documented (consistent with parent AC7 "no net increase in brittle tests" and the epic's "live e2e is overkill / never flakes CI" posture); AC4 asserts round-trip OUTCOMES not whole-blob equality, imposing no brittle expectation on consumers.
- Residual references — BENIGN. The Phase3.verify-inbound-comments-gap NOT-SYNCED guard 822a converts is its live audit target; no sibling deletes the probe scripts under scripts/jira-pressure-test. 33d0's deletion of the dead typed-leaf applier strands nothing 822a references (822a drives the production batch path, not the typed leaf).

The roster-level parent-AC6 absence (documented anti-change-detector guardrail GOVERNING new tests) is a G3 coverage matter already tracked across the sibling set and on this ticket's prior G3 audits — it is not a G4 cross-child inconsistency, so it is not re-filed here.

VERDICT for this pairing (parent × 822a): no G4 cross-child finding; all 7 interaction modes read benign under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter.
